CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Set For Trial On Feb-17-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff ALEXANDER XUE, in pro per present.
Atty. SAMUEL RAY, Esq. of Colla & Ray LLP (tel: 415-579-1414) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to strike defendant Highfield Hospitality, Inc.'s First Amended Verified Answer is denied. Xue asserts uncertainty in which affirmative defenses apply to which cause of action. Because all of the causes of action rely on the same facts, the Amended Answer is most intelligibly read as applying all affirmative defenses to all causes of action. It is appropriate for the Amended Answer to plead by way of affirmative defense that Xue fails to state a claim. A request for attorneys' fees and costs of suit is not required to be asserted by counterclaim.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2025 Continued To Jun-04-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's DEMURRER to ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's demurrer to the answer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to defenses 1 through 10. Defendant merely alleges terse conclusions of law-not ultimate facts-to support the defenses. (FPI Devel., Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384 [defenses must be "averred as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and are alleged in the complaint."]; Quantification Settlement Agreement Cases (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 758, 813 [same].) Plaintiff correctly notes that (1) defense 2 does not comply with CCP 458 [party pleading statute of limitations defense must cite code section]; and defense 11 (mistitled "Twelfth Affirmative Defense"), which reserves defendant's right to amend to plead unnamed defenses, is unnecessary. The demurrer to defense 11 is sustained without leave to amend

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Continued To Mar-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff ALEXANDER XUE, in pro per present.
Atty. SAMUEL RAY, Esq. of Colla & Ray LLP (tel: 415-579-1414) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court orders as follows:

Plaintiff Alexander Xue is ordered to comply with local rule 2.7B and provide courtesy copies to department 301. The Court continues the matter to March 6, 2025, at 9:00 A.M. in Department 301.

Counsel Samuel Ray is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Apr-09-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S Motion To Deem Facts Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to deem facts admitted is denied for two separate and independent reasons. First, plaintiff prematurely served the requests for admissions. Plaintiff served the discovery on September 17, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 5.) Service of the discovery was premature since plaintiff had yet to effectuate service of the summons and complaint, which occurred on September 22, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 6, Ex. B.) "A plaintiff may make requests for admission by a party without leave of court at any time that is 10 days after the service of the summons on, or appearance by, that party, whichever occurs first." (CCP 2033.020(b).) The claimed service of the original complaint on July 11, 2024,did not comply with CCP 415.20(a). That provision allows a plaintiff to serve a corporation by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint at the office of the agent for service of process, president, etc. or that individual's usual mailing address. (See CCP 416.10.) Here, plaintiff served a bartender at defendant's cocktail bar. (Ray Decl., par. 4.) There is no showing that the cocktail bar is the office or usual mailing address of one of the individuals identified in CCP 416.10. Second, the motion is moot because defendant Highfield Hospitality provided responses on December 13, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 12, Ex. C.) Both sides' request for monetary sanctions are denied since neither side's position lacked substantial justification. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HK)

Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
ALEXANDER XUE, (650) 200-098 youyouxue@gmail.com Plaintiff pro se
Samuel Ray,Esq., Colla & Ray LLP 415-579-1414 sam@collaray.com Attorney for Defendant Highfield Hospitality, Inc.
Teresa Kenworthy, CSR 6673, 530-308-2691, tkenworthy@gmail.com, Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC. Motion To Set Aside Default.

Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on October 23, 2024 is granted per CCP 473(b). The motion is timely as defendant filed it a mere two days after entry of the default. Defendant demonstrates a reasonable excuse. Defendant mistakenly or inadvertently mis-calendared the due date for its response to the complaint. (Ray Decl., par. 10.) There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits (see McClain v. Kissler (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 399, 413) and "very slight evidence will be required to justify the court in setting aside the default." (Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 696.) Defendant therefore meets the requirements for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). Defendant shall file its proposed answer by December 2, 2024.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
